title: Outline
summary: Outline is a mature TypeScript knowledge base with a production MCP server, clean command pattern, and plugin architecture. Primary value as a pattern source for JASON-OS (MCP infrastructure, extensibility) rather than adoption target.
creator_view: Outline understands that team knowledge needs hierarchy, real-time collaboration, and AI-agent access. Its MCP server, command pattern, and plugin system are the highest-value extraction targets. Challenge: SoNash Cloud Functions need a transport-agnostic command layer before MCP becomes cheap.

candidates.name: MCP OAuth scope-filtered tool registration
candidates.description: Production MCP server that filters tool registration based on OAuth scopes. Tools only visible if token has matching scope. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with Zod validation.

candidates.name: Command pattern (transport-agnostic business logic)
candidates.description: Business logic in transport-agnostic command files (~8700 lines, ~20 commands). Both HTTP API routes and MCP tools delegate to same commands. Enables adding new surfaces without duplicating logic.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash Cloud Functions could adopt this to enable future MCP surface

candidates.name: PluginManager hook type registry
candidates.description: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.). Priority-based ordering. Auto-discovery via glob. plugin.json manifests. Structural client/server/shared split per plugin.
candidates.home_connection: JASON-OS skill/agent registration and discovery system

candidates.name: buildAPIContext() MCP-to-API bridge
candidates.description: Bridge function that constructs a Koa-style APIContext from MCP request context. Enables MCP tools to reuse existing API middleware and command infrastructure without duplication.
candidates.home_connection: Pattern for bridging MCP context to existing SoNash Cloud Function infrastructure

candidates.name: @Encrypted field-level decorator pattern
candidates.description: Sequelize model decorator that transparently encrypts/decrypts specific fields using AES via SECRET_KEY. Applied at the model layer, not the application layer.

candidates.name: TestServer ephemeral-port integration testing
candidates.description: Custom test utility that starts a real Koa HTTP server on port 0 for integration tests. Tests exercise middleware, routing, and serialization through the actual transport.
candidates.home_connection: Alternative to SoNash httpsCallable mocking approach

candidates.name: Abstract generic Store<T> with request deduplication
candidates.description: MobX base Store with generic typing, observable data Map, standardized RPC actions, and request dedup via Symbol-keyed promise tracking. Prevents N+1 fetch patterns.
candidates.home_connection: Pattern for SoNash state management if complexity grows beyond React hooks

candidates.name: PersistenceExtension race condition prevention
candidates.description: HocusPocus persistence extension uses SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent race conditions during first-write of collaborative documents. Checks for existing Y.js state before initialization.
candidates.home_connection: General lesson in concurrent write handling applicable to any multi-user feature

candidates.name: Content-as-code onboarding templates
candidates.description: 4 markdown templates in server/onboarding/ injected into new team workspaces. User-facing content stored as code, versioned with the app.

candidates.name: Presenter response formatting layer
candidates.description: JSON response formatting layer between models and API/MCP consumers. Each model has a presenter controlling exposed fields and formatting.
candidates.home_connection: Pattern if SoNash adds formal API surface

candidates.name: Bull queue processor BaseProcessor pattern
candidates.description: Base class for async job processors with ~20 specialized implementations. Event-driven architecture for background work.
candidates.home_connection: Pattern if SoNash needs complex async processing beyond Cloud Functions

creatorLens: Outline understands that team knowledge needs hierarchy, real-time collaboration, and AI-agent access. Its MCP server routes through the same command layer as HTTP — AI access gets the same auth and audit trail. The plugin system with 10 hook types and structural client/server split is the cleanest extensibility pattern analyzed. Challenge: transport-agnostic commands enabled MCP for free.
